textPlain: Desktop memory has also increased in price. I think it’s twice as expensive for DDR5 than it was 6 months ago.
I bet some are already buying the highest capacatiy DDR5 DIMMs in bulk to later put them on eBay in the upcoming major DRAM shortage.
Has the death of Moore's Law been officially announced yet?

> OpenAI's Stargate project to consume up to 40% of global DRAM outputhttps://www.tomshardware.com/pc-components/dram/openais-star...> South Korean SK Hynix has exhausted all of its chip 
production for next year and plans to significantly increase investment, anticipating a prolonged "super cycle" of chips, spurred by the boom of artificial intelligence, it said on Wednesday after reporting a record quarterly profit.https://en.ilsole24ore.com/art/korean-chip-race-sk-hynix-has...> Adata chairman says AI datacenters are gobbling up hard drives, SSDs, and DRAM alike — insatiable upstream demand could soon lead to consumer shortageshttps://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/big-tech/adata-ch...
Down stream this is driving up DDR4 demand as well :(
Regular Monopoly/Duopoly like the storage market or Nepopoly like the GPU market?Either way, without competition expect it to increase further.
Yep, DDR5 prices have nearly doubled in less than 2 months. https://pcpartpicker.com/trends/price/memory/#ram.ddr5.5200....

I've just built a gaming PC (after more than a decade without one), for curiosity's sake I just compared the prices I paid for DDR5 2 months ago to now, and at my location it already shows a 25-30% increase. Bonkers...
I was looking att filling my EPYC servers empty slots, what I paid $90/stick 2-3 years ago is now $430
